title: Connections
subtitlePrefix: Approved AI clients. Routing (which Space, which Bundle) is configured in
searchPlaceholder: Search by name, alias, or id…

capability.reportsWorkspaceTitle: This client declares the MCP roots capability. Its sessions route via Workspace bindings; the per-client grant list below applies only to rare rootless reconnects.
capability.rootlessTitle: This client does NOT declare the MCP roots capability. It always routes via the per-client grants set in this panel — configure them below.

panel.displayNameHint: An alias shown in logs and this list. Doesn't affect routing.
panel.routingDesc: When this client reports a folder as an MCP root, mcpmux uses the matching Project binding to pick the Space and Bundle.

grants.title: Default for rootless sessions
grants.badgeTitle: Used only when this client connects without reporting a workspace folder
grants.description: This client doesn't declare the MCP roots capability, so its sessions route through the Bundles you pick here instead of through Project bindings. Leaving the list empty denies the client — rootless sessions then see only the built-in mcpmux_* management tools.
grants.noBundles: No Bundles exist in the default Space yet.
grants.noDefaultsWarning: No defaults set — rootless sessions from this client are denied. That's the safe default. Pick a Bundle above only if you trust this client to operate without a workspace folder.

confirm.revokeMessage: Remove "{{name}}"? All tokens for this client will be revoked. The client will need to re-approve to connect again.

gatewayConfirm.title: Gateway port is in use
gatewayConfirm.portTakenMessage: {{sourceLabel}} (:{{port}}) is already taken by another process. Start the gateway on a different port that the system picks automatically? Your IDE configs will need to be updated to point at the new port.
gatewayConfirm.portTakenShortMessage: {{sourceLabel}} (:{{port}}) is already in use. Start on a different port?

onboarding.title: Let's hook up your first IDE
onboarding.intro: mcpmux is one connection your AI client uses to reach every MCP server. Three steps and you're done:
onboarding.step1Title: Pick your IDE below and follow its prompt
onboarding.step1Body: Each card tells you exactly what the button does — either one-click install or copy a small config for you to paste.
onboarding.step2Title: Enable mcpmux in your IDE's MCP settings
onboarding.step2Body: One-click install usually wires it up automatically. If you pasted a config, open your IDE's MCP panel and toggle mcpmux on — a restart may be needed for the IDE to pick it up.
onboarding.step3Body: mcpmux will pop a dialog the moment your IDE reaches the gateway. Until you accept it, nothing is routed.
onboarding.gatewayStoppedBody: Start it from the Dashboard first — otherwise the IDE will hang at initialize.

oauthConsent.errorDesc: Could not process the authorization request

errors.notFound: This authorization request was not found. It may have expired or been processed already.
errors.expired: This authorization request has expired. Please try again from your application.
errors.alreadyProcessed: This authorization request has already been processed.
errors.gatewayUnavailable: The gateway service is not running. Please check that MCPMux is fully started.

oauthConsent.allowDesc: It will be able to call tools you enable for this folder.
